overview: Omni-Tell is a five-color Legacy combo deck that leverages Show and Tell to enter the battlefield with massive creatures for zero mana. By pairing this with Omniscience and Flow State, the pilot can cast high-impact spells and threats without paying their mana costs, rapidly overwhelming opponents. The deck aims to assemble its key pieces quickly while maintaining enough interaction to survive until the combo resolves.
win_conditions: Casting Show and Tell to enter a large creature for free
Using Omniscience to cast multiple spells and finish the game
Flow State enabling free spellcasting to bypass mana requirements
Combining free casting effects with big threats for lethal damage

keep_criteria: Hands with Show and Tell and a large creature
Hands with Omniscience or Flow State and cheap interaction
Hands with multiple lands and a combo piece

Keep hands with 2+ lands (preferably fetchable/shockable), 1+ acceleration (Lotus Field, Chrome Mox, Mox Opal, Arcane Signet), and 1+ combo piece (Omniscience, Flow State, Show and Tell) or draw/spell (Brainstorm, Ponder, Preordain, Thoughtseize).
Keep hands with 3+ lands if no acceleration/combo.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking lands or combo pieces
Hands with only removal or utility cards
Hands with high-cost cards and no free casting
Mulligan pure lands, pure removal, or hands with only one land and no acceleration.

early_game: Look for Show and Tell or free casting enablers
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Mox/Chrome Mox, or cast Thoughtseize/Lightning Bolt.
If Lotus Field is in hand, play it untapped if possible (via fetch/shock) or hold for acceleration.
Turn 2: Cast draw spells (Brainstorm/Ponder), cast Omniscience/Flow State if mana allows, or play Show and Tell.
Sequence lands to ensure colored mana for Omniscience (WUBRG) or Flow State (U).
mid_game: Cast Show and Tell to bring out a threat
Activate Omniscience or Flow State to cast spells for free
Apply pressure while protecting combo pieces
Use fetch lands to find shock lands for colored mana when casting Omniscience.
Don't overcommit to one win condition
Manage graveyard carefully if facing Rest in Peace or Graveyard hate.
late_game: Cast multiple spells for free to overwhelm opponent
Use remaining resources to close out the game
Maintain board presence with large creatures
Omniscience + Show and Tell (Thassa's Oracle/Green Sun's Zenith), Omniscience + Emrakul, the Aeons Torn, or Flow State + Brainstorm/Ponder into a win.
When you have Omniscience/Flow State in play, enough mana to cast it, and a win condition in hand or library.
Also commit when the opponent has no interaction left.

key_cards.name: Show and Tell
key_cards.role: Enables free entry of large creatures
key_cards.notes: Prioritize casting this early to establish board presence without mana commitment

key_cards.name: Omniscience
key_cards.role: Allows casting spells without paying mana costs
key_cards.notes: Use to chain multiple spells or cast high-cost threats for free

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.role: Grants free spellcasting until end of turn
key_cards.notes: Ideal for casting a sequence of spells or finishing the game in one turn

sequencing_tips: Cast Show and Tell before activating free casting effects to maximize impact
Hold up interaction to protect combo pieces from removal
Use Flow State or Omniscience on turns with multiple spells to cast
Prioritize lands early to ensure combo pieces can be cast if needed
Omni-Tell is highly sensitive to graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Scavenging Ooze) and counterspells.
Practice sequencing fetch lands and shock lands to cast Omniscience on turn 2 or 3.
Don't forget that Omniscience lets you cast spells from hand for free, so you can cast Show and Tell, then cast a creature from your hand (like Thassa's Oracle) using Omniscience's effect.
Flow State works similarly but only for instants and sorceries.
Be mindful of the stack when casting multiple spells

matchups.advice: Play interaction early and use Show and Tell to outvalue
Protect combo pieces and use free casting to bypass counters
Disrupt their setup and race with free casting
Use Spell Pierce to protect your own spells. Cast Omniscience/Flow State in response to counterspells if you have priority. Use cheap draw spells to dig around. Don't overextend into sweepers.
Early disruption (Thoughtseize, Lightning Bolt) to hit their mana rocks or key spells. Use Dismember/Negate to remove threats. Play around their win conditions by holding up interaction.

It's a race to assemble your combo first. Use Thoughtseize to steal their Omniscience/Flow State/Show and Tell. Prioritize keeping hands with acceleration and draw. Win by going first and assembling faster, or by out-resolving their counterspells.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (Spell Pierce, Flusterstorm, Negate), sweepers, and graveyard hate.
Note if the opponent is playing fast lands or ramp spells.
if they have no interaction, go all-in.
